title: Today's AI Brief: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action
summary: Google's AI updates: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5, and a vibe-coded quiz in Google AI Studio.

imageSourceTitle: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I've been digging into Google's latest AI updates, and I've got some interesting developments to share. The company has just released 9 demos of their new Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 models in action. I'm excited to dive into what these demos show and what they mean for the future of AI.

First up, let's talk about these demos. Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 are designed to showcase the ability to reason and create. With Gemini Omni, Google's AI can generate content that feels more natural and intuitive. Meanwhile, Gemini 3.5 is built to help developers and users interact with AI in more practical ways. I've taken a look at the demos, and what stands out to me is how seamlessly they integrate into everyday tasks. For instance, one demo shows how Gemini can help with writing and research, making it a potentially powerful tool for creators and professionals.

Another demo that caught my eye is the use of Gemini in educational settings. It seems Google is exploring ways to make learning more interactive and engaging with AI. I'm curious to see how these tools will evolve and become more widely available.

9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action is also worth a quick mention. My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
